content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance · · Administration · · Executive/Legal · · Enforcement · · Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5660         322-5901          322-6441              322-6444

SUPERSEDED
December 2, 1983                                                by: 95-201

Ed Duggan
PT&T
140 New Montgomery Street, Ste. 613
San Francisco 94105

                                                Re: FPPC No. A-83-267

Dear Mr. Duggan:

     This is to confirm the advice I provided to you on
the telephone regarding the reporting of gifts.

     In the situation you described, a company which is
a lobbyist employer gave a gift to a legislative official.
The official did not want to accept the gift, and instead
wanted to pay the company the fair market value of the item.
Your questions were:

     (1)  Is the company required to report the gift on
its Report of Lobbyist Employer?

     (2)  Is the official required to report receipt of
the item on his Statement of Economic Interests?

I advised you that:

     (1)  If the company receives the fair market value of
the item before the end of the calendar month in which the
gift was given, the company is not required to report making
the gift on its Report of Lobbyist Employer.

     (2)  If the legislative official pays the fair market
value of the item within 30 days of receipt, the official
is not required to report the item as a gift on his Statement
of Economic Interests.

     For your information, enclosed is an article entitled,
"Proper Valuation and Reporting of Gifts" (FPPC Bulletin,
November 1, 1982).

Ed Duggan
November 30, 1983
Page Two

     Thank you for your inquiry.  Please call me if you have
any questions.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Jeanne Pritchard
                                        Political Reform Consultant

sections.question: Is the company required to report the gift on its Report of Lobbyist Employer? Is the official required to report receipt of the item on his Statement of Economic Interests?
sections.conclusion: If the company receives the fair market value of the item before the end of the calendar month in which the gift was given, the company is not required to report making the gift on its Report of Lobbyist Employer. If the legislative official pays the fair market value of the item within 30 days of receipt, the official is not required to report the item as a gift on his Statement of Economic Interests.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a lobbyist employer company must report a gift to a legislative official on its lobbyist employer report, and whether the official must report receipt of the item on their Statement of Economic Interests, in a situation where the official refuses the gift and offers to pay fair market value instead.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The company need not report the gift if it receives fair market value payment before month-end, and the official need not report the item if they pay fair market value within 30 days of receipt.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a confirmation letter of prior telephone advice. Questions and conclusion sections are clearly stated but not formally labeled as 'QUESTION' and 'CONCLUSION' headers. The date shows 'December 2, 1983' but references 'November 30, 1983' on page two. FPPC No. A-83-267 matches the document ID provided.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Is the company required to report the gift on its Report of Lobbyist Employer? Is the official required to report receipt of the item on his Statement of Economic Interests?

CONCLUSION: If the company receives the fair market value of the item before the end of the calendar month in which the gift was given, the company is not required to report making the gift on its Report of Lobbyist Employer. If the legislative official pays the fair market value of the item within 30 days of receipt, the official is not required to report the item as a gift on his Statement of Economic Interests.
